rulings.Case Title: SAMIR HINN ET AL VS. DIANA TOM SHARE TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 5, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO TAX COSTS is continued to March 29, 2024 to be heard with other pending motions. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP COOKE VS. ROBERT TAYAC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 5, 2024 line 2. PLAINTIFF PHILIP COOKE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED, subject to Plaintiff obtaining at least a 6-month trial continuance. If the trial is continued Plaintiff shall file and serve the amened complaint within 1 business days of the order granting continuance. In the absence of the trial continuance the prejudice to the defendant precludes the amendment. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: BAYSIDE LASSEN, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. ASIAN, INC., A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 5, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANTS ASIAN, INC., LAMAR HEYSTEK AND FRANK FUNG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Complaint states sufficient facts to support the asserted causes of action. rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC VILLA PROPERTIES, LLC VS. NANCY G. RUBIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant; Memo Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 5, 2024 line 8. PLAINTIFF PACIFIC VILLA PROPERTIES, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY, TO ESTABLISH ADMISSIONS FROM DEFENDANT, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objections to (i) Form Interrogatories - General (Set One), (ii) Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer (Set One), (iii) Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), (iv) Requests for Production of Documents and Tangible Things (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,160. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
